description: _pseudonymize() uses unsalted SHA-256 for GDPR pseudonymization. Without a secret salt/pepper, identical user_ids always produce identical hashes, enabling rainbow table lookups to reverse pseudonymized values in logs.
fix: Add a secret salt (from settings/env var) to the hash: hashlib.sha256((salt + value).encode()).hexdigest()[:32]. Store the salt in Settings as a SecretStr.

description: Exception message f"Classification failed: {e}" is stored in pipeline state errors list. Internal exception details (stack traces, library names, file paths) could leak to downstream consumers (logs, API responses, Streamlit dashboard in Phase 7).
fix: Use a generic message for state: 'Classification failed: internal error'. Log the full exception separately via logger.exception() (already done on line 89) which is only visible in structured logs.
